Image¶

In [2]:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

import astropy.io.fits as fits
from astropy.wcs import WCS
from astropy import units as u

Download a FITS images from the The STScI Digitized Sky Survey¶

  • RA = 18h 01m 40s
  • Dec = -22d 58m 19s
  • Size: 45 x 45 arcminutes
  • Survey: POSS2 Red
  • Download a FITS images of your object
  • Upload the FITS of your object to this directory

Read in the FITS file¶

In [3]:
fits_file = './300fits'
In [4]:
fits.info(fits_file)

Filename: ./300fits
No.    Name      Ver    Type      Cards   Dimensions   Format
  0  PRIMARY       1 PrimaryHDU     161   (2673, 2679)   int16   
  1  xs.mask       1 TableHDU        25   1600R x 4C   [F6.2, F6.2, F6.2, F6.2]
In [5]:
data   = fits.getdata(fits_file, 0)
header = fits.getheader(fits_file, 0)

Get the World Coordinate System (WCS) data from your FITS file.¶

In [6]:
wcs = WCS(header)

WARNING: FITSFixedWarning: 'datfix' made the change 'Set MJD-OBS to 48506.397222 from DATE-OBS'. [astropy.wcs.wcs]

Create the following 5 plots:¶

  • Make them each a seperate plot.
  • All images should have RA and DEC coords on the labels (use WCS)
  • Put a title on each plot
    • Plot 1: A histogram of the pixel values of the target image (label the axes)
    • Plot 2: Target image
    • Plot 3: Target image where (values < MEAN set to min)
    • Plot 4: Target image where (values < (60% of MAX) set to min)
    • Plot 5: Target image where (values < (90% of MAX) set to min)

Actually change the values in the images. Don't use vmin/vmax¶

In [21]:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize = (8, 8), subplot_kw = {'projection':wcs}, constrained_layout = True)

fig.set_constrained_layout_pads(wspace = .15)

ax.set_xlabel("Image Pixels Values", fontfamily = 'serif', fontsize = 20)

ax.set_ylabel("Number of Pixels",fontfamily = 'serif', fontsize = 20)

ax.set_title("Plot 1: Histogram of pixel values", fontfamily = 'serif', fontsize = 20)

plt.hist(data.flatten(), bins = 100);
In [23]:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize = (8, 8), subplot_kw = {'projection':wcs}, constrained_layout = True)

fig.set_constrained_layout_pads(wspace = .15)

ax.set_xlabel("RA (deg)", fontfamily = 'serif', fontsize = 20)

ax.set_ylabel("Dec (deg)",fontfamily = 'serif', fontsize = 20)

ax.set_title("Plot 2: My Image of FITS object", fontfamily = 'serif', fontsize = 20)

ax.imshow(data, cmap=plt.cm.gray);
In [24]:
#Copying the data to avoid vmin/vmax
copy_data = np.copy(data)
copy_data.min(), copy_data.mean(), copy_data.max()
Out[24]:
(np.int16(434), np.float64(6242.348271399659), np.int16(26678))
In [26]:
#Plot 3
copy_data[copy_data < copy_data.mean()] = copy_data.min()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize = (8, 8), subplot_kw = {'projection':wcs}, constrained_layout = True)

fig.set_constrained_layout_pads(wspace = .15)

ax.set_xlabel("RA (deg)", fontfamily = 'serif', fontsize = 20)

ax.set_ylabel("Dec (deg)",fontfamily = 'serif', fontsize = 20)

ax.set_title("Plot 3: My Image of FITS object with constraint on data", fontfamily = 'serif', fontsize = 20)

ax.imshow(copy_data, cmap=plt.cm.gray);
In [29]:
#Plot 4
copy_data[copy_data < .6 * copy_data.mean()] = copy_data.min()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize = (8, 8), subplot_kw = {'projection':wcs}, constrained_layout = True)

fig.set_constrained_layout_pads(wspace = .15)

ax.set_xlabel("RA (deg)", fontfamily = 'serif', fontsize = 20)

ax.set_ylabel("Dec (deg)",fontfamily = 'serif', fontsize = 20)

ax.set_title("Plot 4: My Image of FITS object with constraint on data", fontfamily = 'serif', fontsize = 20)

ax.imshow(copy_data, cmap=plt.cm.gray);
In [30]:
#Plot 5
copy_data[copy_data < .9 * copy_data.mean()] = copy_data.min()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize = (8, 8), subplot_kw = {'projection':wcs}, constrained_layout = True)

fig.set_constrained_layout_pads(wspace = .15)

ax.set_xlabel("RA (deg)", fontfamily = 'serif', fontsize = 20)

ax.set_ylabel("Dec (deg)",fontfamily = 'serif', fontsize = 20)

ax.set_title("Plot 5: My Image of FITS object with constraint on data", fontfamily = 'serif', fontsize = 20)

ax.imshow(copy_data, cmap=plt.cm.gray);
In [ ]:
